summary: Empty d0rk3r sdist pulls undocumented 'request-cache-py' transitive on install
details: The d0rk3r 1.0.8 sdist ships no Python source (no setup.py, no __init__.py, no d0rk3r_pkg/ directory) despite pyproject.toml declaring setuptools to find packages under d0rk3r_pkg*; the declared `d0rk3r` console_script points at a non-existent `d0rk3r_pkg.cli:main`. The only effect of `pip install d0rk3r` is therefore dependency resolution. pyproject.toml line 34 declares a runtime dependency on `request-cache-py>=1.0.1` that is absent from requirements.txt (which lists only `requests[socks]>=2.25.0` and `beautifulsoup4>=4.9.0`) and unmentioned in the README. The name is one character off from the well-known `requests-cache` package (singular vs plural), matching the shape of dependency-confusion / typosquat-via-transitive. Whether installer harm actually occurs depends on the contents of `request-cache-py` itself, which is not part of this artifact and was not analyzed here.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
